name: Orlando Coin Exchange
description_short: Family-owned numismatic business in Orlando specializing in buying and selling coins, precious metals, jewelry, and collectibles. Operates by appointment only with 20+ years of industry experience.
description_long:
Orlando Coin Exchange is a family-owned and operated numismatic business that has served Central Florida and customers nationwide for over 20 years. The company was founded by professionals with deep expertise in coin and currency collecting, and has built a reputation as a trusted dealer within the numismatic community. In 2017, the business transitioned from walk-in service to appointment-only operations to preserve the personalized, family-oriented service that distinguishes it from larger commercial operations.

The company buys and sells a wide range of numismatic and precious metal items, including U.S. and world coins, paper currency, bullion, gold and silver jewelry, diamonds, antiques, and flatware. They position themselves as competitive buyers and sellers, claiming to pay the highest prices in Central Florida for collections of any size or quality. The business maintains memberships with major industry organizations including the American Numismatic Association and Florida United Numismatists, and holds authorizations as a dealer for NGC, PCGS, and PMG grading services.

What distinguishes Orlando Coin Exchange is its emphasis on expertise, personalization, and long-term customer relationships. The company reports that over 75% of its business comes from repeat customers or referrals. They attend major coin shows and auctions, maintain membership in large dealer networks, and actively work to fulfill customer want lists. The appointment-only model reflects a deliberate choice to prioritize service quality over transaction volume, contrasting with typical high-volume pawn operations.

However, the appointment-only requirement is a significant operational constraint for spontaneous sellers or buyers. The business does not offer the immediate cash advances or quick transactions typical of traditional pawn shops, instead positioning itself as a specialized numismatic dealer. Hours are limited (M-F 10am-4:30pm, Sat 10am-3pm), and prospective customers must call ahead to schedule visits. While the company holds a BBB A+ rating, there is no publicly available information about pricing transparency, loan terms, or typical buyback percentages.
pros: BBB A+ rating and 20+ years of documented family ownership and operation
Authorized dealer for NGC, PCGS, and PMG—major third-party coin grading services
Life memberships in American Numismatic Association and Florida United Numismatists
Reports 75% of business from repeat customers and referrals, indicating customer retention
Explicitly claims to pay highest prices in Central Florida and maintains nationally competitive pricing
Will evaluate collections of any size or quality without discrimination
Membership in major dealer networks and attendance at major coin shows for sourcing and sales
cons: Appointment-only model eliminates walk-in convenience and requires advance scheduling by phone
Limited operating hours (closed Sundays, closes at 4:30pm weekdays) reduce accessibility
No published pricing guides, buyback percentages, or loan terms available on website
Specialized numismatic focus means they may not accept items outside coins, metals, and jewelry
No information about whether they offer loans against collateral or only outright purchase/sale
best_for: Serious coin and numismatic collectors seeking expert appraisals and want-list fulfillment
Sellers with significant coin, bullion, or jewelry collections seeking competitive buyout prices
Customers in Central Florida willing to plan ahead and schedule appointments for personalized service
Buyers seeking professionally graded coins from authorized NGC, PCGS, or PMG dealers
diagnosis: Orlando Coin Exchange is best for experienced numismatists and serious collectors with valuable coin or precious metal holdings who prioritize expert evaluation and personalized service over convenience. The critical caveat is the appointment-only model—prospective customers must call (407) 730-3116 to schedule a visit, making this unsuitable for spontaneous sellers or those seeking immediate cash transactions typical of traditional pawn shops.
services: Buy and sell U.S. paper money and currency
Buy and sell world coins and paper money
Buy and sell bullion and precious metals
Buy and sell gold and silver jewelry and watches
Buy and sell silver flatware and serving trays
Buy and sell antiques and Americana
NGC (Numismatic Guaranty Company) authorized dealer services
PCGS (Professional Coin Grading Service) authorized dealer services
PMG (Paper Money Guaranty) authorized dealer services
CAC (Certified Acceptance Corporation) submission center services
